description: Validation checklist for Codex to run after adding rules, tests, or skills. All checks must pass before committing to main.

checks.name: JSON schema validity — all training files
checks.description: Parse each training JSON file and verify it is valid JSON with no trailing commas, comments, or encoding errors.
checks.command: node -e "['rules_advanced','test_suite','automation_skills','fallback_contract','vision_prompts','gap_analysis','training_loop','synthetic_data_factory','validation_checklist'].forEach(f => { try { JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('training/' + f + '.json', 'utf8')); console.log('OK: ' + f); } catch(e) { console.error('FAIL: ' + f + ' — ' + e.message); process.exit(1); } })"
checks.pass_condition: All files parse without error
checks.fail_action: Fix the JSON syntax error in the reported file before proceeding

checks.name: Rule ID uniqueness check
checks.description: Verify that all rule IDs in rules_advanced.json are unique. No two rules may share the same id field.
checks.command: node -e "const r = require('./training/rules_advanced.json'); const ids = r.rules.map(x => x.id); const dupes = ids.filter((v,i) => ids.indexOf(v) !== i); if(dupes.length) { console.error('FAIL: duplicate IDs', dupes); process.exit(1); } else console.log('OK: all rule IDs unique');"
checks.pass_condition: Zero duplicate IDs
checks.fail_action: Rename the duplicate rule ID — use the next available sequence number in the appropriate category prefix

checks.name: Rule schema field completeness
checks.description: Every rule in rules_advanced.json must have: id, pattern, detection_logic (with functions[] and conditions[]), category, confidence (number 0–1), fix_type (safe|needs_review), requires_llm (boolean). bad_dax and fixed_dax are required unless requires_llm is true.
checks.command: node -e "const r = require('./training/rules_advanced.json'); const required = ['id','pattern','detection_logic','category','confidence','fix_type','requires_llm']; let fail=0; r.rules.forEach(rule => { required.forEach(f => { if(rule[f] === undefined) { console.error('FAIL: rule', rule.id, 'missing field', f); fail++; } }); if(!rule.requires_llm && !rule.fixed_dax) { console.error('FAIL: rule', rule.id, 'missing fixed_dax (required when requires_llm=false)'); fail++; } if(!rule.detection_logic?.functions || !rule.detection_logic?.conditions) { console.error('FAIL: rule', rule.id, 'detection_logic missing functions or conditions'); fail++; } }); if(fail) process.exit(1); else console.log('OK: all rule schemas valid');"
checks.pass_condition: Zero field errors across all rules
checks.fail_action: Add missing fields to reported rules before promoting to final_rules.json

checks.name: Confidence value range check
checks.description: All rule confidence values must be between 0.70 and 1.00 inclusive. Rules below 0.70 must be requires_llm: true.
checks.command: node -e "const r = require('./training/rules_advanced.json'); let fail=0; r.rules.forEach(rule => { if(rule.confidence < 0 || rule.confidence > 1) { console.error('FAIL:', rule.id, 'confidence out of range:', rule.confidence); fail++; } if(rule.confidence < 0.70 && !rule.requires_llm) { console.error('FAIL:', rule.id, 'confidence below 0.70 but requires_llm is false'); fail++; } }); if(fail) process.exit(1); else console.log('OK: all confidence values valid');"
checks.pass_condition: Zero range violations
checks.fail_action: Set requires_llm: true for any rule below confidence 0.70, or raise confidence if detection logic is sound

checks.name: Duplicate detection — pattern string similarity
checks.description: Check for rules whose pattern strings share more than 60% word overlap with another rule in the same category. Flag for human review.
checks.command: node -e "const r = require('./training/rules_advanced.json'); const words = s => new Set(s.toLowerCase().split(/\\W+/).filter(w => w.length > 3)); const overlap = (a, b) => { const wa = words(a), wb = words(b); let shared = 0; wa.forEach(w => { if(wb.has(w)) shared++; }); return shared / Math.min(wa.size, wb.size); }; let warn=0; const rules = r.rules; for(let i=0;i<rules.length;i++) for(let j=i+1;j<rules.length;j++) { if(rules[i].category === rules[j].category) { const sim = overlap(rules[i].pattern, rules[j].pattern); if(sim > 0.6) { console.warn('WARN: possible duplicate:', rules[i].id, 'vs', rules[j].id, 'similarity:', sim.toFixed(2)); warn++; } } } if(warn) console.log(warn + ' potential duplicates flagged — review before promoting'); else console.log('OK: no high-similarity rule pairs found');"
checks.pass_condition: Zero pairs with similarity > 0.8 in same category; review all > 0.6 pairs
checks.fail_action: Merge overlapping rules or narrow the detection logic to differentiate them clearly

checks.name: Duplicate detection — function set overlap
checks.description: No two rules in the same category should share ALL detection functions AND more than 2 identical conditions. Such rules are indistinguishable at runtime.
checks.command: node -e "const r = require('./training/rules_advanced.json'); let fail=0; const rules = r.rules; for(let i=0;i<rules.length;i++) for(let j=i+1;j<rules.length;j++) { if(rules[i].category !== rules[j].category) continue; const fi = new Set(rules[i].detection_logic?.functions || []); const fj = new Set(rules[j].detection_logic?.functions || []); const allMatch = [...fi].every(f => fj.has(f)) && [...fj].every(f => fi.has(f)); if(!allMatch) continue; const ci = rules[i].detection_logic?.conditions || []; const cj = rules[j].detection_logic?.conditions || []; const sharedConds = ci.filter(c => cj.includes(c)); if(sharedConds.length >= 2) { console.error('FAIL: rules', rules[i].id, 'and', rules[j].id, 'share all functions and', sharedConds.length, 'conditions — may be duplicates'); fail++; } } if(fail) process.exit(1); else console.log('OK: no indistinguishable rule pairs');"
checks.pass_condition: Zero fully overlapping rule pairs
checks.fail_action: Add distinguishing conditions or merge the rules into one with a broader pattern

checks.name: Test coverage — every rule has at least one positive test
checks.description: Every rule ID in rules_advanced.json must appear in at least one positive_cases entry in test_suite.json.
checks.command: node -e "const rules = require('./training/rules_advanced.json').rules; const suite = require('./training/test_suite.json'); const covered = new Set(suite.positive_cases.map(t => t.expected_rule).filter(Boolean)); let fail=0; rules.forEach(r => { if(!covered.has(r.id)) { console.error('FAIL: rule', r.id, 'has no positive test case'); fail++; } }); if(fail) process.exit(1); else console.log('OK: all rules have positive test coverage');"
checks.pass_condition: Zero uncovered rules
checks.fail_action: Add a positive test case to test_suite.json for each uncovered rule ID

checks.name: Test coverage — every rule has at least one negative test
checks.description: Every rule ID must appear as a referenced rule in at least one negative_cases entry, confirming a similar-looking valid DAX does not trigger it.
checks.command: node -e "const rules = require('./training/rules_advanced.json').rules; const suite = require('./training/test_suite.json'); const negRules = new Set(suite.negative_cases.map(t => t.rule_must_not_fire).filter(Boolean)); let warn=0; rules.forEach(r => { if(!negRules.has(r.id)) { console.warn('WARN: rule', r.id, 'has no negative test — false positive risk'); warn++; } }); if(warn) console.log(warn + ' rules lack negative tests — add before promoting to high-confidence'); else console.log('OK: all rules have negative test coverage');"
checks.pass_condition: Zero uncovered rules (warnings acceptable, failures are not)
checks.fail_action: Add a negative test for each uncovered rule, especially for safe fix_type rules

checks.name: Test coverage — every rule has at least one edge case
checks.description: Every rule ID should appear in at least one edge_cases entry to prevent false positives on boundary conditions.
checks.command: node -e "const rules = require('./training/rules_advanced.json').rules; const suite = require('./training/test_suite.json'); const edgeRules = new Set(suite.edge_cases.map(t => t.rule_id).filter(Boolean)); let warn=0; rules.forEach(r => { if(!edgeRules.has(r.id)) { console.warn('WARN: rule', r.id, 'has no edge case'); warn++; } }); console.log(warn + ' rules lack edge cases');"
checks.pass_condition: All high-confidence (>=0.90) rules have edge cases; others are warned
checks.fail_action: Add edge cases for any rule with confidence >= 0.90 that lacks one

checks.name: Rule compilation dry run
checks.description: Simulate the rule compiler by loading all rule files, deduplicating, and generating the compiled output structure. Verify output has same rule count minus duplicates.
checks.command: node -e "const adv = require('./training/rules_advanced.json').rules; const ids = new Set(); const compiled = []; adv.forEach(r => { if(!ids.has(r.id)) { ids.add(r.id); compiled.push({id: r.id, category: r.category, confidence: r.confidence, fix_type: r.fix_type, requires_llm: r.requires_llm, functions: r.detection_logic.functions}); } }); const byCategory = {}; compiled.forEach(r => { byCategory[r.category] = (byCategory[r.category]||0)+1; }); console.log('Compiled rules:', compiled.length); console.log('By category:', JSON.stringify(byCategory)); console.log('OK: compilation dry run complete');"
checks.pass_condition: Compiled rule count equals input count minus exact duplicates; no errors thrown
checks.fail_action: Fix any rule that causes a compilation error — typically a missing required field or invalid fix_type value

checks.name: Full test runner — positive cases
checks.description: For each positive test case in test_suite.json, simulate running the rule engine against the input DAX and verify the expected rule fires. Uses function-string pattern matching as a proxy for the full engine.
checks.command: node -e "const rules = require('./training/rules_advanced.json').rules; const suite = require('./training/test_suite.json'); let pass=0, fail=0; suite.positive_cases.forEach(tc => { const rule = rules.find(r => r.id === tc.expected_rule); if(!rule) { console.error('FAIL: test', tc.name, 'references unknown rule', tc.expected_rule); fail++; return; } const dax = (tc.input||'').toUpperCase(); const allFnsPresent = rule.detection_logic.functions.every(fn => dax.includes(fn.toUpperCase())); if(allFnsPresent) { pass++; } else { const missing = rule.detection_logic.functions.filter(fn => !dax.includes(fn.toUpperCase())); console.error('FAIL: test', tc.name, '— rule', rule.id, 'would not fire; missing functions:', missing); fail++; } }); console.log('Positive tests passed:', pass, '/ failed:', fail); if(fail > 0) process.exit(1);"
checks.pass_condition: All positive test cases have all expected rule functions present in the DAX
checks.fail_action: Update the test case DAX to include the triggering functions, or update the rule detection logic if the test is correct

checks.name: Full test runner — negative cases
checks.description: For each negative test case in test_suite.json, verify the named rule would NOT fire against the input DAX (i.e., at least one required function is absent from the input).
checks.command: node -e "const rules = require('./training/rules_advanced.json').rules; const suite = require('./training/test_suite.json'); let pass=0, fail=0; suite.negative_cases.forEach(tc => { if(!tc.rule_must_not_fire) return; const rule = rules.find(r => r.id === tc.rule_must_not_fire); if(!rule) { console.warn('WARN: negative test', tc.name, 'references unknown rule', tc.rule_must_not_fire); return; } const dax = (tc.input||'').toUpperCase(); const allFnsPresent = rule.detection_logic.functions.every(fn => dax.includes(fn.toUpperCase())); if(!allFnsPresent) { pass++; } else { console.error('FAIL: negative test', tc.name, '— rule', rule.id, 'would fire but should not'); fail++; } }); console.log('Negative tests passed:', pass, '/ failed:', fail); if(fail > 0) process.exit(1);"
checks.pass_condition: Zero negative test cases where the named rule would fire
checks.fail_action: Either narrow the rule detection conditions to exclude valid patterns, or fix the negative test to correctly not contain all rule functions

checks.name: Browser engine parity test
checks.description: Verify that every action type referenced in automation_skills.json has a corresponding handler in contentScript.js. Check: click, type, key, scroll, wait, screenshot, vision, drag, right_click, double_click.
checks.command: node -e "const skills = require('./training/automation_skills.json'); const fs = require('fs'); const cs = fs.readFileSync('src/contentScript.js', 'utf8'); const actionTypes = new Set(); skills.forEach(s => s.actions.forEach(a => actionTypes.add(a.type))); const missing = []; actionTypes.forEach(t => { const patterns = {'click':'clickAtCoord\\|findAndActElement','type':'typeIntoField\\|execCommand','key':'pressKeyCombo\\|KeyboardEvent','scroll':'scrollPage\\|scrollBy','wait':'setTimeout\\|wait(','screenshot':'captureScreenshot\\|TAKE_SCREENSHOT','vision':'callVisionApi','drag':'mousemove\\|left_mouse','right_click':'contextmenu\\|right_click','double_click':'dblclick\\|isDouble'}; const pattern = patterns[t]; if(pattern && !new RegExp(pattern).test(cs)) { missing.push(t); console.error('FAIL: action type', t, 'has no handler in contentScript.js'); } }); if(missing.length) process.exit(1); else console.log('OK: all action types have handlers');"
checks.pass_condition: All action types in automation_skills.json have corresponding code in contentScript.js
checks.fail_action: Implement the missing action handler in contentScript.js before the skill can be used

checks.name: Synthetic DAX coverage — all expected rules triggered
checks.description: For each domain in synthetic_data_factory.json, verify that every rule in expected_rules_triggered exists in rules_advanced.json.
checks.command: node -e "const factory = require('./training/synthetic_data_factory.json'); const rules = new Set(require('./training/rules_advanced.json').rules.map(r => r.id)); let fail=0; factory.domains.forEach(d => { (d.expected_rules_triggered||[]).forEach(rid => { if(!rules.has(rid)) { console.error('FAIL: domain', d.domain, 'expects rule', rid, 'which does not exist in rules_advanced.json'); fail++; } }); }); if(fail) process.exit(1); else console.log('OK: all synthetic domain rule references are valid');"
checks.pass_condition: All referenced rule IDs exist in rules_advanced.json
checks.fail_action: Either add the missing rule to rules_advanced.json or update the domain expected_rules_triggered to reference the correct rule ID

checks.name: False positive audit — edge cases must not trigger their named rule
checks.description: For each edge case in test_suite.json that has rule_must_not_fire set, run function-matching and confirm the rule would not fire.
checks.command: node -e "const rules = require('./training/rules_advanced.json').rules; const suite = require('./training/test_suite.json'); let pass=0, fail=0; (suite.edge_cases||[]).forEach(tc => { if(!tc.rule_must_not_fire) return; const rule = rules.find(r => r.id === tc.rule_must_not_fire); if(!rule) return; const dax = (tc.input||'').toUpperCase(); const allFnsPresent = rule.detection_logic.functions.every(fn => dax.includes(fn.toUpperCase())); if(!allFnsPresent) { pass++; } else { console.error('FAIL: edge case', tc.name, '— rule', rule.id, 'fires but should be suppressed by context'); fail++; } }); console.log('Edge case false-positive check passed:', pass, '/ failed:', fail); if(fail > 0) process.exit(1);"
checks.pass_condition: Zero edge cases where rule fires but should not
checks.fail_action: Add a distinguishing condition to the rule detection_logic to prevent false positive on this edge case

checks.name: Confidence adjustment after false positives
checks.description: For every rule that fires on a negative test case (from val-012), reduce its confidence by 0.05. If confidence drops below 0.70, set requires_llm: true automatically.
checks.command: node -e "console.log('This check must be run AFTER val-012 reports failures.'); console.log('For each failing rule: reduce confidence by 0.05 in rules_advanced.json.'); console.log('If new confidence < 0.70: set requires_llm to true and remove from deterministic execution.'); console.log('Re-run val-004 and val-012 after adjustments.');"
checks.pass_condition: All rules adjusted and re-validated pass val-004 and val-012
checks.fail_action: Apply confidence reductions and re-run the full checklist from val-001

checks.name: Screenshot workflow test
checks.description: Verify that background.js has the TAKE_SCREENSHOT message handler and that contentScript.js calls it correctly with the captureScreenshot function.
checks.command: node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const bg = fs.readFileSync('src/background.js', 'utf8'); const cs = fs.readFileSync('src/contentScript.js', 'utf8'); const checks = [['background.js has TAKE_SCREENSHOT handler', bg.includes('TAKE_SCREENSHOT')], ['background.js returns dataUrl', bg.includes('dataUrl')], ['background.js calls captureVisibleTab', bg.includes('captureVisibleTab')], ['background.js returns true for async', bg.includes('return true')], ['contentScript.js has captureScreenshot function', cs.includes('captureScreenshot')], ['contentScript.js sends TAKE_SCREENSHOT message', cs.includes(\"type: 'TAKE_SCREENSHOT'\" ) || cs.includes('TAKE_SCREENSHOT')]]; let fail=0; checks.forEach(([desc, result]) => { if(!result) { console.error('FAIL:', desc); fail++; } else console.log('OK:', desc); }); if(fail) process.exit(1);"
checks.pass_condition: All 6 screenshot workflow checks pass
checks.fail_action: Ensure background.js has the TAKE_SCREENSHOT listener with return true, and contentScript.js uses sendMessage with type TAKE_SCREENSHOT

checks.name: Action planner dry run
checks.description: Verify that contentScript.js has all required action planner functions: callActionPlanApi, renderActionPlan, runApprovedSteps, executeStep.
checks.command: node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const cs = fs.readFileSync('src/contentScript.js', 'utf8'); const required = ['callActionPlanApi','renderActionPlan','runApprovedSteps','executeStep','callVisionApi']; let fail=0; required.forEach(fn => { if(!cs.includes(fn)) { console.error('FAIL: function', fn, 'not found in contentScript.js'); fail++; } else console.log('OK:', fn); }); if(fail) process.exit(1); else console.log('OK: all action planner functions present');"
checks.pass_condition: All 5 action planner functions exist in contentScript.js
checks.fail_action: Implement the missing function according to the gap_analysis.json and fallback_contract.json specifications

checks.name: Safety approval test — model-modifying steps require approval
checks.description: In the fallback_contract.json example action_plan, verify that every step with type 'type' or 'click' targeting a save/commit action has requires_approval: true.
checks.command: node -e "const contract = require('./training/fallback_contract.json'); const plan = contract.example_response.action_plan; let fail=0; plan.forEach((step, i) => { const isModelModifying = step.type === 'type' || (step.type === 'click' && (step.target.includes('Commit') || step.target.includes('Save') || step.target.includes('Publish') || step.target.includes('Delete') || step.target.includes('Overwrite'))); if(isModelModifying && !step.requires_approval) { console.error('FAIL: step', i+1, '(', step.type, step.target, ') modifies the model but requires_approval is false'); fail++; } }); if(fail) process.exit(1); else console.log('OK: all model-modifying steps in example action plan require approval');"
checks.pass_condition: Zero model-modifying steps without requires_approval: true
checks.fail_action: Set requires_approval: true on any step that types DAX, saves, or commits a measure change

checks.name: RLS rule safety check
checks.description: All rules that detect RLS-related patterns (USERNAME, USERPRINCIPALNAME, REMOVEFILTERS on fact, ALL(fact)) must have fix_type: needs_review and requires_user_approval or requires_llm: true.
checks.command: node -e "const rules = require('./training/rules_advanced.json').rules; let fail=0; const rlsKeywords = ['USERNAME','USERPRINCIPALNAME','RLS','security','REMOVEFILTERS','ALL(fact']; rules.forEach(r => { const isRLS = rlsKeywords.some(kw => (r.pattern||'').toLowerCase().includes(kw.toLowerCase()) || (r.category||'').toLowerCase().includes('rls')); if(isRLS && r.fix_type === 'safe' && !r.requires_llm) { console.error('FAIL: rule', r.id, 'is RLS-related but fix_type is safe — must be needs_review or requires_llm'); fail++; } }); if(fail) process.exit(1); else console.log('OK: all RLS-related rules are marked needs_review or requires_llm');"
checks.pass_condition: Zero RLS-related rules with fix_type: safe
checks.fail_action: Change fix_type to needs_review and set requires_llm: true for all rules touching security filters, USERPRINCIPALNAME, or REMOVEFILTERS on fact tables

checks.name: Manifest permissions check
checks.description: Verify manifest.json includes all required permissions for the Act tab features: activeTab, tabs, storage, scripting.
checks.command: node -e "const m = require('./manifest.json'); const required = ['activeTab','tabs','storage','scripting']; let fail=0; required.forEach(p => { if(!(m.permissions||[]).includes(p)) { console.error('FAIL: manifest missing permission:', p); fail++; } else console.log('OK: permission', p); }); if(fail) process.exit(1);"
checks.pass_condition: All 4 required permissions present in manifest.json
checks.fail_action: Add the missing permission to the permissions array in manifest.json

checks.name: Vision prompt ID uniqueness
checks.description: All vision prompt IDs in vision_prompts.json must be unique.
checks.command: node -e "const vp = require('./training/vision_prompts.json'); const ids = vp.map(v => v.id); const dupes = ids.filter((v,i) => ids.indexOf(v) !== i); if(dupes.length) { console.error('FAIL: duplicate vision prompt IDs:', dupes); process.exit(1); } else console.log('OK: all vision prompt IDs unique');"
checks.pass_condition: Zero duplicate vision prompt IDs
checks.fail_action: Rename duplicate vision prompt IDs using the next available vision-NNN sequence

checks.name: Automation skill ID uniqueness and action completeness
checks.description: All automation skills must have unique skill names, and each must have at least one action with a type field.
checks.command: node -e "const skills = require('./training/automation_skills.json'); const names = skills.map(s => s.skill); const dupes = names.filter((v,i) => names.indexOf(v) !== i); if(dupes.length) { console.error('FAIL: duplicate skill names:', dupes); process.exit(1); } let fail=0; skills.forEach(s => { if(!s.actions || s.actions.length === 0) { console.error('FAIL: skill', s.skill, 'has no actions'); fail++; } s.actions.forEach((a,i) => { if(!a.type) { console.error('FAIL: skill', s.skill, 'action', i, 'has no type'); fail++; } }); }); if(fail) process.exit(1); else console.log('OK: all automation skills valid');"
checks.pass_condition: Zero duplicate skill names; all actions have type fields
checks.fail_action: Add missing type fields or rename duplicate skills

checks.name: Training loop schema check
checks.description: Verify training_loop.json has all required top-level keys: training_loop (array), failure_capture_schema (object), coverage_metrics (array), promotion_criteria (array), fallback_criteria (array).
checks.command: node -e "const tl = require('./training/training_loop.json'); const required = ['training_loop','failure_capture_schema','coverage_metrics','promotion_criteria','fallback_criteria']; let fail=0; required.forEach(k => { if(!tl[k]) { console.error('FAIL: training_loop.json missing key:', k); fail++; } else console.log('OK:', k); }); if(fail) process.exit(1);"
checks.pass_condition: All 5 required keys present
checks.fail_action: Add the missing key to training_loop.json

checks.name: Coverage summary report
checks.description: Generate a summary coverage report: total rules, rules with positive tests, rules with negative tests, rules with edge cases, requires_llm count, safe count, needs_review count.
checks.command: node -e "const rules = require('./training/rules_advanced.json').rules; const suite = require('./training/test_suite.json'); const positiveRules = new Set(suite.positive_cases.map(t => t.expected_rule).filter(Boolean)); const negRules = new Set(suite.negative_cases.map(t => t.rule_must_not_fire).filter(Boolean)); const edgeRules = new Set((suite.edge_cases||[]).map(t => t.rule_id).filter(Boolean)); const total = rules.length; const withPos = rules.filter(r => positiveRules.has(r.id)).length; const withNeg = rules.filter(r => negRules.has(r.id)).length; const withEdge = rules.filter(r => edgeRules.has(r.id)).length; const llm = rules.filter(r => r.requires_llm).length; const safe = rules.filter(r => r.fix_type === 'safe').length; const review = rules.filter(r => r.fix_type === 'needs_review').length; const avgConf = (rules.reduce((s,r) => s + r.confidence, 0) / total).toFixed(3); console.log('--- Coverage Report ---'); console.log('Total rules:', total); console.log('With positive test:', withPos, '(' + (withPos/total*100).toFixed(1) + '%)'); console.log('With negative test:', withNeg, '(' + (withNeg/total*100).toFixed(1) + '%)'); console.log('With edge case:', withEdge, '(' + (withEdge/total*100).toFixed(1) + '%)'); console.log('Requires LLM:', llm); console.log('Safe fix:', safe); console.log('Needs review:', review); console.log('Avg confidence:', avgConf);"
checks.pass_condition: Report generated successfully; positive test coverage >= 80%; zero rules without fix_type
checks.fail_action: Add missing tests or fix rules with undefined fix_type

run_order: val-001 — JSON validity (gate: all other checks depend on this)
val-002, val-003, val-004 — rule schema and field checks
val-007, val-008, val-009 — test coverage checks
val-011, val-012 — positive and negative test runner
val-015, val-016 — false positive audit and confidence adjustment
val-017, val-018 — screenshot and action planner workflow
val-019, val-020 — safety approval and RLS checks
val-021, val-022, val-023, val-024 — extension manifest and schema checks

pass_threshold.note: Blocking checks must all pass before merging to main. Warning-only checks should be resolved within 2 sprint cycles.
